title: Lumen Field — Primary World Cup Venue
content: Lumen Field is the primary FIFA World Cup 2026 venue in Seattle, located at 800 Occidental Ave S in the SoDo neighborhood. The stadium seats approximately 69,000 fans and is home to the Seattle Seahawks (NFL) and Seattle Sounders FC (MLS). For the World Cup, the field will be converted to a natural grass surface to meet FIFA standards. The stadium is served by the SoDo Station on Link Light Rail and is a 15-minute walk from Pioneer Square. Major transit connections include the Link 1 Line, multiple King County Metro bus routes, and the Seattle Streetcar. Parking is limited and expensive on match days — public transit is strongly recommended.

title: Getting to Seattle — Airport and Transit Options
content: Seattle-Tacoma International Airport (SEA) is the main gateway, located about 14 miles south of downtown. The Link 1 Line light rail connects the airport to downtown in approximately 40 minutes for $3.25. Rideshare (Uber/Lyft) costs $35-55 to downtown depending on traffic. The airport also has rental car facilities in a garage connected by a shuttle. From downtown, the Link Light Rail runs directly to SoDo Station near Lumen Field. King County Metro buses cover the greater Seattle area, and the Washington State Ferries connect to Bainbridge Island and other Puget Sound destinations. Water taxis also run from the downtown waterfront to West Seattle.

title: Getting Around Seattle During the World Cup
content: Seattle's public transit system includes Link Light Rail, King County Metro buses, the Seattle Streetcar, and Washington State Ferries. An ORCA card works across all systems and costs $3 per ride on most routes. On match days, expect increased frequency on the Link 1 Line between Westlake and SoDo stations. Rideshare pickup zones will be designated near Lumen Field but expect surge pricing. Cycling is popular — Seattle has extensive bike lanes, and Lime bikes are available for rent via app. Walking is feasible in many neighborhoods: Pike Place Market to Pioneer Square is about 15 minutes on foot. For fans staying in Bellevue or the Eastside, the Link 2 Line connects to downtown Seattle.

title: Best Food Near Lumen Field
content: The SoDo and Pioneer Square neighborhoods near Lumen Field offer many dining options. Popular spots include: Taqueria La Fondita (Mexican street food, 5-minute walk), Altstadt (German-style beer hall and sausages on 1st Ave S), and Japonessa (upscale sushi in Pioneer Square). For quick bites, the food trucks on Occidental Ave are a match-day staple — expect Thai, Filipino, BBQ, and taco options. Royal Brewery and Elysian Fields offer craft beer close to the stadium. For a more upscale pre-game meal, try Il Corvo (handmade pasta, Pioneer Square) or Taylor Shellfish Oyster Bar (waterfront). Most restaurants within a 10-minute walk of the stadium will have extended hours on match days.

title: Seattle's Must-Try Food Experiences
content: No visit to Seattle is complete without Pike Place Market — the 116-year-old public market is home to the original Starbucks, Pike Place Fish Co. (famous for fish throwing), and dozens of local food vendors. Must-tries include Beecher's Handmade Cheese (mac and cheese), Piroshky Piroshky (Russian pastries), and Ellenos Real Greek Yogurt. Beyond the market, Seattle is known for its coffee culture — try Victrola, Elm Coffee Roasters, or Storyville for local roasts. The International District (Chinatown) has incredible dim sum at Jade Garden and pho at Than Brothers. Capitol Hill is the city's dining hub with restaurants like Canon (whiskey bar), Stateside (Vietnamese), and Altura (Italian tasting menu).

title: Seattle Weather During the World Cup (June–July 2026)
content: Contrary to its rainy reputation, Seattle summers are generally dry and pleasant. June and July average highs of 70-75°F (21-24°C) with lows around 55°F (13°C). Rain is unlikely but not impossible — expect maybe 3-4 rainy days per month in summer. The sun doesn't set until about 9:15 PM in late June, giving long evenings perfect for exploring the city after matches. UV levels can be moderate to high, so sunscreen is recommended for outdoor activities. Layers are key: mornings and evenings can be cool, while afternoons are warm. If you're attending an evening match, bring a light jacket. Seattle rarely gets extreme heat, but occasional heat waves can push temperatures into the 85-90°F range.

title: Where to Stay for the World Cup in Seattle
content: Downtown Seattle is the most convenient base, with direct light rail access to Lumen Field. Top hotel options include the Hyatt Regency Seattle (next to the Convention Center), Hotel 1000 (boutique, downtown), and the Edgewater Hotel (waterfront, iconic). Budget travelers should consider hotels in the University District (U-District), which has light rail access and cheaper rates. Capitol Hill has great Airbnb options and is the city's nightlife hub. For groups, renting a house in Ballard or Fremont gives a local neighborhood feel with good bus connections. Pioneer Square hotels like the Best Western Plus are closest to the stadium but the neighborhood can be rough at night. Bellevue across Lake Washington is another option with the new Link 2 Line providing access to downtown and SoDo. Book early — expect World Cup pricing premiums of 2-3x normal rates.

title: Top Things to Do in Seattle Beyond the World Cup
content: Seattle offers world-class attractions for visitors. The Space Needle ($35-40 admission) offers 360-degree views from its rotating glass floor at 520 feet. Chihuly Garden and Glass, located next to the Space Needle, showcases stunning glass art installations. The Museum of Pop Culture (MoPOP) features interactive music, sci-fi, and pop culture exhibits. For nature lovers, take the Bainbridge Island Ferry (35 minutes, walk-on fare ~$9) for a scenic Puget Sound crossing with views of the Olympic Mountains. Kerry Park in Queen Anne provides the classic Seattle skyline photo with Mount Rainier in the background. The Seattle Waterfront and Pier 57 Great Wheel offer casual strolling and Ferris wheel rides. Discovery Park has 534 acres of trails and a historic lighthouse. For day trips, Mount Rainier National Park is 2.5 hours away and offers spectacular hiking.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Ticket Information for Seattle Matches
content: Tickets for FIFA World Cup 2026 matches in Seattle are sold through FIFA's official ticketing platform at FIFA.com. Ticket categories range from Category 1 (best seats, $200-300 for group stage) to Category 3 (upper sections, $70-120 for group stage). Knockout round tickets are significantly more expensive. A FIFA Fan ID is required for all ticket holders and serves as entry permission to the host country for international visitors. The secondary market on StubHub and SeatGeek will also have tickets, but prices vary widely. For the best value, apply during FIFA's official ticket sales phases — first-come, first-served and lottery-based allocation. Accessibility tickets are available for fans with disabilities. Each ticket holder can purchase up to a defined limit per match to prevent scalping.

title: Safety Tips for World Cup Visitors in Seattle
content: Seattle is generally a safe city for tourists, but standard precautions apply. The downtown core, Pioneer Square, and 3rd Avenue near Pike Street can have street-level issues — stay aware of your surroundings, especially at night. Keep valuables secure and avoid leaving items visible in parked cars. Emergency services: dial 911. The non-emergency police line is 206-625-5011. Seattle has a strong culture of inclusivity and welcomes international visitors. Tap water is safe to drink — Seattle's water comes from mountain reservoirs and is excellent. Cannabis is legal in Washington State for adults 21+ and can be purchased at licensed dispensaries. Tipping is customary at restaurants (18-20%) and for rideshare/taxi drivers (15-20%). The U.S. dollar is the local currency; credit cards are accepted almost everywhere.
